firms.notes: US futures prop firm (Bulenox LLC, Wilmington DE, founded 2022). Two-stage model: pay a monthly fee (~$145 for 25K up to $535 for 250K; frequent 50-75% coupons) for a Qualification (evaluation) account, hit the profit target with no minimum trading days, then convert to a Master (funded) account with a one-time activation fee ($143 on 25K up to $898 on 250K). Trader picks the drawdown mechanic at signup: Option 1 'No Scaling' = real-time TRAILING drawdown that follows the account high-water mark (including unrealized), or Option 2 'EOD' = end-of-day drawdown plus fixed daily loss limits. In both cases the drawdown stops trailing once it reaches starting balance +$100 (i.e. locks at breakeven+$100, not full profit). Max drawdown $1,500-$5,500 and profit target $1,500-$15,000 by size; max contracts 3-25 by size. Payouts: min 10 individual trading days on the Master account, min $1,000 withdrawal, processed weekly on Wednesdays, 90% split (first $10,000 of profit paid at 100% with no commission, 10% firm fee thereafter). A 40% consistency rule applies at payout (no single day may exceed 40% of total profit) and there's a non-withdrawable safety-threshold reserve (e.g. $3,100 on 100K) plus first-3-payout caps ($1,000-$2,500 by size). Positions must be flat before session close (no overnight holds). Platform: free NinjaTrader 8 license routing on a Rithmic data feed. 100+ restricted countries (China, Iran, Russia, North Korea, Syria, Cuba, Venezuela, etc.). Trust signals: Trustpilot ~4.7-4.8/5 across ~1,300-1,670 reviews ('Excellent'), strong payout-reliability reputation, ~4 years operating. | [2026-07-02] Automation-friendly: Bulenox explicitly permits EAs/algos/bots and trade copiers without disclaimers; a 3rd-party bot connects via NinjaTrader 8 + Rithmic (or bridges like CrossTrade/PickMyTrade for TradingView webhooks, ~30ms), so trade-scope API automation is highly feasible — but copy trading ACROSS multiple accounts and cross-account hedging are prohibited, and true HFT (mass order spam/latency arbitrage) is banned.

firms.name: Mubite
firms.payoutDays: 14 (Instant: first payout 14 days after first trade, then bi-weekly); Challenge funded: on-demand
firms.cryptoAssets: 700+ Bybit USDT-perpetual pairs (BTC, ETH + altcoins), crypto only; up to 100x. Real Bybit order-book execution on firm-allocated simulated/demo capital (Bybit demo environment, api-demo.bybit.com)
firms.notes: Crypto-native, Bybit-integrated prop firm (Mubite s.r.o., Skolska 660/3, Prague, Czech Republic; reg. 23221551; launched Oct 2024, CEO Peter Andreas). Three tracks: Instant Funding ($1,250-$100,000, no profit target, 70-80% split), One-Step ($5k-$200k, 10% target, 4% daily / 6% max DD, 80-90% split) and Two-Step ($5k-$200k, 10%+5% target, 5% daily / 8% max DD, 80-90% split). Trades route through Bybit's real order-book liquidity but on firm-allocated simulated/demo capital (Bybit demo-trading env, api-demo.bybit.com) - trader connects a Bybit account via API; Bybit-restricted regions (US, UK, Canada, France, Singapore, Hong Kong, plus sanctioned states) instead use Mubite's proprietary 'Cleo' web platform. Daily drawdown = 5% of equity at midnight-UTC reset (static, equity-based incl. floating PnL); Instant max DD tightens 10%->5% after +5% profit. Scaling ladder $10k->$20k->$40k->$80k->$160k (10% per 120 days, half to payout / half to grow) up to $1M total. Payouts: Instant first payout 14 days after first trade then bi-weekly, Challenge funded = on-demand; processed 12-48h after invoice approval; settlement in USDT/crypto (bank transfer also cited); ~5% payout cap per request; min trading days ~4 (firm's own rules page also cites 13 - conflict). Stop-loss NOT mandatory (recommended). News trading, weekend/overnight holds allowed; banned: HFT/latency arbitrage, tick-scalping with manipulative intent, cross-account hedging, group/mirror trading. Copy trading fully prohibited; bots allowed ONLY if self-developed (must prove authorship) - third-party/public bots banned; Cleo platform 'does not support external bots/third-party automation'. Trust signals mixed: Trustpilot ~4.1 (96 reviews) but documented payout-denial complaints (rules enforced at payout, not during) plus Czech COI / ECC-Net complaints; unclaimed/unverified on thetrustedprop (60/100). | [2026-07-02] A trade-scope Bybit (demo) API key on the funded account is the same technical rail as Hyrotrader so a bot CAN physically execute, but Mubite's Trader Agreement bans third-party/public bots AND copy/signal replication and enforces at payout - so a TradeSurge-style third-party execution bot is contractually prohibited (feasibility LOW despite the working API).

automation.platform: Bybit (native, via trade-scope API) for supported regions; proprietary 'Cleo' web platform for Bybit-restricted regions (US/UK/CA/FR/SG/HK/sanctioned states)
automation.copy: Banned - copy trading prohibited in both evaluation and funded phase (Trader Agreement breach)
automation.note: Trade-scope Bybit demo-account API key = same technical rail as Hyrotrader, so a 3rd-party bot CAN physically place orders on the funded account. BUT rules allow only self-built bots (must prove authorship); third-party/public bots + copy/signal trading are explicitly banned, and the Cleo platform 'does not support external bots/third-party automation'. Enforcement happens at payout (documented denials). A TradeSurge-style third-party execution/relay bot is contractually prohibited -> high payout-forfeiture risk despite a working API.
